channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India-US Trade Deal Delays as Officials Reject Rush to Agreement
item.description: Union Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal has dismissed reports suggesting India is delaying a US trade deal, calling the claims 'completely false'. The minister clarified that both nations remain committe...

item.title: 15 Disney stars and their struggles with substance abuse and rehab
item.description: The article highlights the challenges faced by 15 former Disney stars who struggled with substance abuse and sought rehabilitation. It details their careers, the pressures of fame, and the impact of t...

item.title: Tanzania to Develop Petroleum Refining to Boost Economy
item.description: Tanzania is advancing plans to establish petroleum refining capacity, marking a key step in processing crude oil into refined products domestically. This shift aims to reduce reliance on imported refi...

item.title: Malaysia and Singapore to Finalize JS-SEZ Master Plan Soon
item.description: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced that Malaysia and Singapore will finalize the master plan for the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) in the near future. During a courtesy...

item.title: Pag-IBIG Encourages Developers to Lower Home Prices with Financing Support
item.description: The Philippine Housing Finance Corporation (Pag-IBIG) has issued a directive to housing developers, urging them to reduce home prices in order to make homeownership more accessible. The agency emphasi...

item.title: PTI Supports Aleema’s Protest While Cautioning Against False-Flag Risks
item.description: The Pakistan Tehreek-i-Insaf (PTI) has publicly endorsed Aleema Khan's call for a 'long march' protest, but senior leaders have expressed concerns that the demonstration could escalate into another 'f...

item.title: Former Government Scientist Faces Charges Over Alleged Data Transfer to China
item.description: A former Canadian government scientist, Dennis Lu, faces criminal charges after allegedly downloading thousands of work files from the Department of Natural Resources to external storage devices, pote...

item.title: Graduates in Mozambique Struggle with Job Market Realities
item.description: The article discusses how young Africans, particularly in Mozambique, are entering the workforce but often end up in jobs that don't align with their education or aspirations. Despite high economic ac...

item.title: Fable Café Blends Art and Dining After One Year of Operation
item.description: Fable Café, a unique dining concept located at the Grand Hyatt Manila in BGC, has been operating for one year. The café continues to challenge traditional boundaries by merging artistic expression wit...

item.title: Australian Defence Contract with Thales Faces Audit Concerns Over Tender Process and Compliance Issues
item.description: The Australian government's $1.9 billion munitions contract with Thales, a partially French state-owned defence company, has come under scrutiny following a damning audit report. The contract, signed ...

item.title: Xabi Alonso and Andoni Iraola Set Ambitious Goals for Chelsea and Liverpool
item.description: Two Spanish managers, Xabi Alonso and Andoni Iraola, have outlined their vision for success as they take charge of Chelsea and Liverpool respectively. Alonso, newly appointed as Chelsea manager, empha...

item.title: South Africa's Ramaphosa Extends Condolences to Qatar Following Former Leader's Passing
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has extended his condolences to the Royal Family, government, and people of Qatar following the death of His Highness Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, the former Amir of Qa...

item.title: Montreal Police Complete Second Day of Search for Missing Man in St. Lawrence River
item.description: Montreal police have concluded the second day of a search for a missing man in the St. Lawrence River, following a multi-agency effort to locate the individual. The search, which began after the man w...

item.title: China and Namibia Strengthen Bilateral Ties Through Strategic Cooperation
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Namibian President Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah held high-level talks in Beijing, agreeing to elevate their bilateral relationship by fostering a shared future for developme...

item.title: How to Pay Kenya Power Bills via M-Pesa and Avoid Common Errors
item.description: Kenya Power customers can now pay their electricity bills through M-Pesa by following specific steps. Prepaid users receive a token code to load onto their meters, while postpaid customers must clear ...

item.title: University professor fears masked intruders during antisemitism inquiry
item.description: A University of Melbourne physics professor has recounted his experience of discovering approximately 20 masked intruders in his campus office, describing the moment of uncertainty about whether the i...

item.title: UN Experts Condemn India's Voter Deletion Practices Targeting Muslims
item.description: Three United Nations special rapporteurs have raised serious concerns about India's mass voter deletion process, alleging that millions of voters—particularly Muslims—were wrongfully removed from elec...

item.title: Edmonton deals with sinkhole and flooding aftermath after severe storms
item.description: A massive sinkhole measuring 12x12 feet and over 10 meters deep has formed near the intersection of 130 Avenue and 107 Street in Edmonton's Lauderdale neighborhood following intense weekend thundersto...

item.title: Oriire school abduction: Children beaten, teachers chained as rescue operation succeeds
item.description: A group of 39 pupils and seven teachers were abducted in Oriire Local Government Area, Oyo State, and held captive for 56 days. The principal, Mrs Racheal Alamu, revealed that children were beaten int...

item.title: Supreme Court Addresses POCSO Misuse and Elopement of Teenagers
item.description: The Supreme Court recently raised concerns about the misuse of POCSO (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences) provisions against teenagers in voluntary sexual relationships. A bench of Justices B...
